brands.name: Level Up Automation
brands.notes: Smart-home/commercial automation services franchise founded 2016 in Northborough, MA, franchising since 2021; very small emerging system (~10 units) with a low-cost, home-based, tech-services model. Directory sources show conflicting fee figures, so current FDD verification is advised.

riskFlags.title: Very small, unproven system
riskFlags.detail: Only about 10 units after roughly 4 years of franchising (franchising since 2021). System performance at scale is unproven and there is no public Item 19 data found.

riskFlags.title: Conflicting fee disclosures across directories
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur lists a $35,000 franchise fee and $51.8K-$68.2K total investment; Franchise Gator-affiliated listings cite a $49,500 fee and $56.3K-$72.7K investment. Prospects should confirm against the current FDD (FDDs are listed on Wefranch).

riskFlags.title: Franchise sales availability appears limited
riskFlags.detail: Franchising.com showed the brand as not currently accepting new applicants, and the brand's own Franchise Gator listing has been noted as at capacity, suggesting paused or selective franchise sales.

franchiseFee.note: Conflict: some aggregator listings report a $49,500 franchise fee; Entrepreneur (typically FDD-derived) lists $35,000

yearFranchising.note: Franchise Gator lists 2020; Entrepreneur and franchise counsel announcement (spadealaw.com) support 2021 launch

veteranDiscount.note: Entrepreneur lists 10% off franchise fee; Franchise Gator lists 15% off

isGrowing.note: +66.7% unit growth per Entrepreneur, but off a very small base (~10 units)

marketingSupport.note: Marketing templates, SEO, website development per Entrepreneur

brands.name: Lightspeed Restoration
brands.notes: Water/fire damage restoration franchise launched in 2023 by Home Franchise Concepts (a JM Family Enterprises subsidiary), headquartered in Flower Mound/Coppell, TX. Very young brand (~38 units) but backed by a large, experienced multi-brand franchisor; fee structure is a $5K franchise fee plus a $44K-$112K territory fee.

riskFlags.title: Very new brand with no long-term track record
riskFlags.detail: Founded and franchising since 2023; only ~38 units. No multi-year unit-economics history exists for franchisee outcomes in this system.

riskFlags.title: Layered ongoing fees and territory pricing
riskFlags.detail: Beyond the $5,000 franchise fee, territory fees run $44,000-$112,000; ongoing costs include a tiered royalty (reported 2%-8%, with $500-$1,000 monthly minimums), 2% brand fund (min $500/mo), and a $750/month technology fee for the first territory (2025 FDD summary).

riskFlags.title: FDD contains litigation/bankruptcy disclosures to review
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz flags that Lightspeed Restoration's FDD discloses lawsuit and/or bankruptcy information (Items 3/4); specifics are not public and should be reviewed in the FDD before purchase. May relate to parent-company history rather than this young brand.

riskFlags.title: Backed by large parent (mitigant)
riskFlags.detail: Part of Home Franchise Concepts, a subsidiary of JM Family Enterprises ($22B annual revenue), which partially offsets typical new-brand risk; brand ranked #32 on Entrepreneur's Top New & Emerging Franchises list.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Range $154,230-$334,000 per 2025 FDD Item 7 (also reported by Franchise Direct)

franchiseFee.note: Initial franchise fee is $5,000 PLUS a territory fee of $44,000-$112,000 (varies by territory demographics); effective minimum entry licensing cost ~$49,000, which is why some aggregators report a $49K fee

royaltyPercent.note: Tiered royalty reported as 2%-8% of sales (other summaries describe it starting at 8% and declining as revenue grows), with monthly minimums of $500 (year 1) / $1,000 thereafter per 2025 FDD

totalUnits.note: Entrepreneur lists 38; Vetted Biz lists 27 and Franchise Direct ~30 (different snapshot dates for a fast-changing young system)

trainingWeeks.note: 12-14 days of required training (third-party certifications plus in-person/virtual instruction at the Coppell, TX Experience Center); Entrepreneur lists 123 classroom + 37 on-the-job hours

veteranDiscount.note: 15% off initial franchise and territory fees for veterans and spouses

multiUnitAvailable.note: FDD prices additional contiguous territories (e.g., reduced $250/mo tech fee per added territory)

isGrowing.note: 0 to ~38 units since 2023 launch; ranked #32 Top New & Emerging Franchises

brands.name: Voda Cleaning & Restoration
brands.notes: Carpet/floor cleaning and water-damage restoration franchise (parent: Franchise Playbook, CEO Dan Claps) built on the DNA Pro Cleaning predecessor business; began franchising in 2023 and sold 100+ territories within a year. Item 19 is anchored on the high-performing affiliate flagship outlet, with franchisee quartile data first appearing in the 2025 FDD.

riskFlags.title: Territories sold far ahead of open units
riskFlags.detail: Brand marketing cites 100+ franchise owners and 260+ locations/territories awarded across 31 states, but the 2025 FDD shows only 58 franchised units open plus 1 company outlet at 2024 year-end. Rapid territory sales out ahead of operating units is a classic emerging-brand execution risk.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur directory claim vs. 2025 FDD data via Franchise Chatter

riskFlags.title: Item 19 anchored on a single affiliate flagship outlet
riskFlags.detail: Headline revenue ($2.4M-$2.7M) comes from the predecessor 'DNA Pro Cleaning' flagship covering ~3 territories with 6 service vans — not a typical franchisee. In the 2025 FDD, the bottom 25% of the 13 reporting franchisees averaged only ~$203K-$280K gross revenue.

riskFlags.title: High combined ongoing fee load
riskFlags.detail: 7% royalty (with monthly minimums) + brand fund 1-2% + required local advertising of the greater of 5% of revenue or $2,500-$5,000/month + $1,000+/month technology fee — a heavier ongoing burden than many home-services peers.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter FDD summary / Entrepreneur directory

totalInvestmentMin.note: Entrepreneur range $206,659-$376,873; 2025 FDD Item 7 per Franchise Chatter was $201,374-$357,608

totalInvestmentMax.note: 2025 FDD per Franchise Chatter shows $357,608 max

royaltyPercent.note: Greater of 7% of gross revenue or $0-$1,500/month minimum

adFundPercent.note: Brand fund: 1% of gross revenue first 12 months, 2% thereafter; separate required local advertising of greater of 5% or $2,500-$5,000/month

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 (2025 FDD): 2024 gross revenue of the affiliate flagship/predecessor outlet (covers ~3 territories, 6 vans) — NOT a franchisee average. Among 13 franchisees open 12+ months, top-25% averaged ~$603K-$1.04M and bottom-25% ~$203K-$280K

totalUnits.note: Entrepreneur 2026 listing; 2025 FDD showed 58 franchised + 1 company-owned at 2024 year-end; marketing cites 260+ territories awarded

yearFounded.note: 2009 reflects the predecessor flagship business (formerly DNA Pro Cleaning); the Voda franchise brand was created ~2022 and began franchising 2023

veteranDiscount.note: Entrepreneur lists veteran incentive as not available

multiUnitAvailable.note: Multi-territory development deals awarded (e.g., 5 territories in Denver)

canRunPartTime.note: Listed as part-time capable by Entrepreneur, but emergency restoration work is typically a full-time commitment

isGrowing.note: Ranked #65 Fastest-Growing and #26 Top New & Emerging (Franchise 500, 2026); 0 to 58+ open franchised units in two years

brands.name: Vicky Bakery
brands.notes: Cuban bakery-cafe brand founded 1972 in Hialeah, FL with a ~50-year family operating history, but only franchising since 2021 (~27 units, concentrated in South Florida). High investment ($627K-$1.2M) and steep liquidity requirements ($500K liquid / $750K net worth) relative to its short franchising track record.

riskFlags.title: High investment and liquidity bar for a young franchise system
riskFlags.detail: Total investment of $626,700-$1,198,700 with $500,000 minimum liquid assets and $750,000 net worth required, while the brand has only franchised since 2021 — franchisee-level economics outside the founding family network are largely unproven. No public Item 19 earnings data was found.
riskFlags.source: Vicky Bakery official franchise page / Entrepreneur directory

riskFlags.title: Geographic concentration in South Florida
riskFlags.detail: Nearly all ~27 locations are in the greater Miami / South Florida market; the concept's Cuban bakery model is untested in other regions, concentrating market and hurricane-exposure risk.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum net worth $750,000 also required (franchisor's own franchise page)

totalInvestmentMin.note: Entrepreneur: $626,700-$1,198,700; SharpSheets cites a lower $439K floor from an earlier FDD

totalUnits.note: Up from 25 in 2024 (+50% growth over recent period per Entrepreneur)

trainingWeeks.note: Derived from 120 total training hours (101 on-the-job + 19 classroom) per Entrepreneur

fddAvailable.note: Franchisor's site directs prospects to review the FDD after initial inquiry

isGrowing.note: 25 units (2024) to 27 units; Entrepreneur reports +50% growth over its measured period

brands.name: Just Poke
brands.notes: Seattle-area fast-casual poke bowl chain founded 2016 (HQ Bellevue, WA; founder Norman Wu), franchising since 2020 with ~32-36 locations concentrated in the Puget Sound region plus British Columbia expansion. Low-overhead model (no full kitchens), but it remains a regional emerging brand with no public Item 19 data found.

riskFlags.title: Regional emerging brand, unproven outside Pacific Northwest
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since 2020 with ~32-36 units, nearly all in the Puget Sound / Seattle region (with early BC, Canada expansion). The concept's performance outside its home market is unproven, and the crowded poke segment has seen other chains struggle.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory / justpoke.com franchising page

riskFlags.title: No public Item 19 / earnings data found
riskFlags.detail: No FDD Item 19 financial performance representations for Just Poke could be located in public franchise databases; prospective buyers must obtain the FDD directly to evaluate unit economics.
riskFlags.source: Web search of FDD aggregators (Vetted Biz, Franchise Chatter, franchimp)

totalUnits.note: Entrepreneur lists 36 (+176.9% growth); franchisor site says 32 restaurants open by 2024 with 40+ targeted by 2026

fddAvailable.note: Actively franchising in the US since 2020 (Entrepreneur directory listing is FDD-derived)

veteranDiscount.note: 50% off franchise fee for veterans per Entrepreneur

isGrowing.note: 32 restaurants open by 2024 including British Columbia expansion; targeting 40+ by 2026

marketingSupport.note: Co-op advertising, social media, SEO, grand-opening support

ongoingSupport.note: Site selection, lease negotiation, continuous training per franchisor site
